title: 2026 FIFA World Cup: Round of 16 Predictions
preparedNote: Bracket, team news, cards, injuries, venues and weather verified against sources as of July 4, 2026. For entertainment, not betting advice.
howToRead: 'Chance to advance' includes extra time and penalties. Two yellow cards across matches means a one-match ban; yellows reset after the quarterfinals.
projectedQuarterfinals: QF1: France vs Morocco, Boston (pairing of ties 1-2 confirmed by sources)
QF2: Brazil vs Mexico (pairing of ties 3-4 confirmed by sources)
QF3: Spain vs USA (pairing of ties 5-6 implied by bracket/schedule)
QF4: Argentina vs Colombia (pairing of ties 7-8 implied by bracket/schedule)

matches.rationale: Quality, pedigree and a perfect shootout record beat home energy
matches.sheetTitle: Match 1: Canada vs Morocco, Sat July 4, 2026

sections.name: Prediction
rows: Morocco's superior quality and tournament pedigree edge out Canada's home energy and extra rest

sections.name: Match Info
rows: Houston Stadium (NRG Stadium), Houston, TX, capacity 72,220
Retractable roof with full air-conditioning (one of only 3 climate-controlled venues); temporary natural grass tray system
Outside: 35-37C (mid-to-upper 90sF), heat index 100-107F, mainly dry with slight storm chance. Roof expected closed with A/C, so match conditions are controlled, heat neutralized
Winner faces France/Paraguay winner in Boston (quarterfinal)

sections.name: Team Comparison
rows: Aggressive high-energy pressing, counters through Jonathan David and wide pace (Buchanan, Millar)
Patient build-up through Saibari between the lines, Brahim Diaz in support, Hakimi overlapping from right back; strong on set pieces and corners
2nd in Group B (4 pts): 1-1 Bosnia, 6-0 Qatar (first-ever WC win, David hat-trick), 1-2 Switzerland
2nd in Group C (7 pts): 1-1 Brazil, 1-0 Scotland, 4-2 Haiti, unbeaten
1-0 South Africa (Eustaquio stoppage-time volley), first-ever knockout win
1-1 Netherlands, won 3-2 on penalties (Bounou save; Saibari winning kick)
W2 D1 L1, scored in every game, riding emotion of historic first knockout win
Unbeaten (W3 D2 incl. shootout), African champions, 2022 semifinalists, growing into the tournament
Jonathan David: 3 goals incl. hat-trick vs Qatar (first CONCACAF hat-trick since 1930)
Ismael Saibari: 3 goals, new Bayern Munich signing, scored winning penalty vs Netherlands
Stephen Eustaquio (R32 winner), Tajon Buchanan, Alphonso Davies (back from injury)
Achraf Hakimi, Brahim Diaz, Yassine Bounou (shootout hero)
Nathan Saliba, Niko Sigur: one more booking = miss the QF
Ismael Kone OUT (broken ankle); Alphonso Davies fit again, could make first start
Played June 28 in LA, extra day of rest, no extra-time legs
Played 120 min + shootout June 29 in Monterrey; travels from Mexico with heavier legs
Never contested a World Cup shootout
Never lost one: beat Spain (2022) and Netherlands (2026)

sections.name: Head-To-Head
rows: 4 meetings: Morocco 3 wins, 1 draw, Canada winless. Last meeting: Morocco 2-1 at the 2022 World Cup group stage

sections.name: Boosts and Hindrances (Pros / Cons)
rows: Co-host support and big traveling fanbase; extra day of rest; no extra-time fatigue; momentum from first-ever knockout win; Davies returning
Higher quality squad (rank 6 vs 30); unbeaten all tournament; huge Moroccan fan presence in Houston (semi-home game); perfect shootout record; H2H dominance
Never beaten Morocco; lost their toughest group game (Switzerland); Kone out; two players one booking from QF ban
120 minutes + shootout 5 days ago plus travel from Monterrey; conceded in 3 of 5 games; Diop on a yellow

sections.name: Market and Expert View
rows: Moneyline: Morocco -125/-135, Canada +450/+460, Draw +250 (RotoWire, FOX). Opta supercomputer: Morocco 52.7% in 90 min, Canada 21.7%, extra time 25.6%. RotoWire projects Morocco 2-1

sections.name: Final Verdict
rows: MOROCCO 2-1. Canada's rest edge and crowd make it competitive, but Morocco have more quality in every line, the better tournament pedigree, and the H2H edge. Fatigue is the only real doubt. If it goes to penalties, Morocco's perfect shootout record decides it anyway
Al Jazeera preview; Sky Sports match reports; Squawka suspension tracker; RotoWire odds; KPRC/FOX 26 Houston weather; Fanorate NRG Stadium guide

matches.rationale: Best team in the tournament vs the weakest attack left
matches.sheetTitle: Match 2: France vs Paraguay, Sat July 4, 2026

sections.name: Prediction
rows: The tournament's best team against its biggest underdog. Class gap too wide, even in brutal heat

sections.name: Match Info
rows: Philadelphia Stadium (Lincoln Financial Field), Philadelphia, PA, open-air, ~69,000 (NFL config; WC config unconfirmed)
No roof, fully exposed; natural grass installed for the tournament

~37C (98F) in a record East Coast heat wave; thunderstorms possible (France's earlier match here was rain-delayed 2 hours). Misting fans and cooling tents for fans; both teams keen to avoid extra time
Played on the 250th anniversary of the Declaration of Independence, in the city where it was signed. Winner faces Canada/Morocco in Boston (QF)

sections.name: Team Comparison
rows: Uncharacteristically attacking Deschamps side, interchangeable front three (Mbappe, Dembele/Olise, Barcola), intricate passing, little reliance on crosses
Deep defensive block, physical, transitions via Enciso/Almiron and set pieces; keeper Gill in inspired form
Won Group I (9 pts): 3-1 Senegal, 3-0 Iraq, 4-1 Norway (Dembele 32-min hat-trick, 2nd fastest in WC history)
3rd in Group D (4 pts), best third-place qualifier: 1-4 USA, 1-0 Turkey (fastest goal of tournament, 64 sec), 0-0 Australia
1-1 Germany, won 4-3 on penalties (Gill saved from Havertz and Woltemade), national holiday declared in Paraguay
4 wins from 4, widely rated the best team of the tournament, chasing a 3rd straight final
W1 D2 L1, defense-first survival football; first World Cup in 16 years
Kylian Mbappe: 6 goals, joint Golden Boot leader; 18 career WC goals, one behind Messi's all-time 19
Julio Enciso: 1 goal, the attacking spark; keeper Orlando Gill the shootout hero
Michael Olise (creative star), Ousmane Dembele (hat-trick vs Norway), Barcola, Doue
Matias Galarza, Miguel Almiron, Andres Cubas
Cubas and Galarza; coach Alfaro also booked (staff subject to same rule)
Enciso listed as probable; carry 120 min + shootout in the legs
Played June 30; based in NY/NJ-Philly corridor all tournament, already won at this stadium (vs Iraq)
Played June 29 plus extra time; travel from Boston
Just won one vs Germany; the one scenario Paraguay would relish

sections.name: Head-To-Head
rows: 5 meetings: France 3 wins, 2 draws, Paraguay winless. Includes 1998 WC R16: France 1-0 (golden goal) with Deschamps as captain, en route to the title. 2017 friendly: France 5-0

sections.name: Boosts and Hindrances (Pros / Cons)
rows: Best attack in the tournament (13 goals); Mbappe in Golden Boot form; venue familiarity and minimal travel; extra day of rest; no card risks
Nothing-to-lose mentality and national euphoria; elite shootout keeper (Gill); heat and possible storms are chaos factors that help underdogs
37C open-air heat could flatten their pressing and tempo; storm delay risk; complacency against a deep block
Weakest attack left in the tournament (0.36 xG/game); extra-time legs + travel; Cubas/Galarza one card from QF bans; never beaten France

sections.name: Market and Expert View
rows: France -550, Paraguay +1400, Draw +600; Over 2.5 goals -150 (Covers/Kalshi). Opta: France 78.8% in 90 min, Paraguay 7.6%. Covers pick: France -2.5 (+130)

sections.name: Final Verdict
rows: FRANCE 3-0. The largest mismatch of the round. Paraguay's only path is a 0-0 into penalties, and France's firepower (3+ goals in every game) makes that very unlikely even in the heat. France win in 90 minutes
Al Jazeera preview; Covers odds; Wikipedia Group D/I; Copa America recap; Squawka tracker; Billy Penn / FOX 29 Philadelphia heat coverage

matches.venue: New York New Jersey Stadium (E. Rutherford)
matches.rationale: Rest + depth finally break the Norway hex; Haaland scores anyway
matches.sheetTitle: Match 3: Brazil vs Norway, Sun July 5, 2026

sections.name: Prediction
rows: Brazil's depth and rest edge out Haaland's history-making Norway, but Brazil have never beaten them

sections.name: Match Info
rows: New York New Jersey Stadium (MetLife), East Rutherford, NJ, capacity ~82,500, open-air, sea level
No roof; temporary natural grass laid over artificial turf
Hot and humid: highs 32-34C (low 90sF), showers/thunderstorms possible, regional flash-flood risk. Afternoon kickoff in full heat
Winner faces Mexico/England winner in the quarterfinal

sections.name: Team Comparison
rows: 4-3-3 (shifted to 4-2-4 chasing the game vs Japan)
Direct, transition-heavy through Haaland; Odegaard and Berg as creators
Possession with Vini Jr/Rayan wide; Casemiro-Guimaraes spine; blown hot and cold
All-out attack, defensively leaky, scored AND conceded in every match
Won Group C (7 pts): 1-1 Morocco, 3-0 Haiti, 3-0 Scotland (Vini Jr brace)
2nd in Group I (6 pts): 4-1 Iraq (Haaland brace), 3-2 Senegal (Haaland x2), 1-4 France (rotated XI), first WC since 1998
2-1 Japan, trailed 1-0, Casemiro equalizer, Martinelli winner in 90+5
2-1 Ivory Coast, Haaland winner in the 86th; Norway's first-ever WC knockout win
D-W-W-W but unconvincing; needed a comeback vs Japan; earliest exit since 1990 looms if they lose
W-W-L-W with huge momentum in their first knockouts in 28 years
Vinicius Junior: 4 goals + 1 assist, Brazil's standout
Erling Haaland: 5 goals (half of Norway's 10); fastest ever to 60 international goals (60 in 53 caps); scored in every competitive Norway game since Oct 2024
Matheus Cunha (3 goals), Casemiro, Guimaraes, Martinelli
Martin Odegaard (3 assists), Patrick Berg (2 assists), Sorloth, Nusa
Danilo and Casemiro: one more booking = miss the QF
Raphinha and Paqueta ruled OUT; Casemiro expected to play
Ryerson (thigh) close to return; Haaland fully fit
5 full days rest (played June 29), short trip from Houston
3 days rest (played July 1) plus travel from Texas
Never contested a World Cup shootout

sections.name: Head-To-Head
rows: Brazil have NEVER beaten Norway, 0 wins in 4 meetings, most famously Norway 2-1 at France '98. Last met in a 1-1 friendly (2006)

sections.name: Boosts and Hindrances (Pros / Cons)
rows: More rest (5 days vs 3); deeper squad; big Brazilian crowd expected in NJ; heat suits them more than Norway's pressing game; key duel favors Vini Jr vs Norway's leaky defense (8 conceded)
Haaland unstoppable form and physical mismatch vs Marquinhos; zero pressure, all momentum; Brazil's wobbles vs organized opposition; the H2H hex
No Raphinha/Paqueta; Danilo and Casemiro on yellows; slow starts (trailed vs Japan); has never solved Norway
Worst defense left in the tournament (2 conceded per game); 2 fewer rest days plus travel; afternoon heat punishes their high-energy style

sections.name: Market and Expert View
rows: bet365: Brazil -112, Draw +240, Norway +320 (implied ~52/26/22). Popular angles: BTTS (landed in all 4 Norway games), Over 2.5, Vini anytime. Sports Mole boldly predicts 1-2 Norway; most analysts lean Brazil narrowly

sections.name: Final Verdict
rows: BRAZIL 2-1. Norway will score. They always do, and Haaland is the best striker in the tournament. But Norway also concede in every game, Brazil have 2 extra rest days, and the heat blunts Norway's running. Brazil finally break the Norway hex, with extra time a live risk
Sports Mole; Squawka preview + suspension tracker; ESPN/Sky match reports; Man City (Haaland record); compare.bet odds; AccuWeather East Rutherford

matches.rationale: Altitude + fortress Azteca + 4 clean sheets vs England's patched defense
matches.sheetTitle: Match 4: Mexico vs England, Sun July 5, 2026

sections.name: Prediction
rows: A genuine coin flip: altitude, the Azteca fortress and Mexico's perfect defense tip it against England's stuttering form

sections.name: Match Info
rows: Sunday, July 5, 2026, approx 6:00 PM local Mexico City (8:00 PM ET / 1:00 AM BST Mon)
Mexico City Stadium (Estadio Azteca), capacity ~87,000 post-renovation; ALTITUDE ~2,200m (7,220 ft)
Rainy season: mild ~24C (76F) high, 14C low; evening thunderstorms/showers a strong possibility at kickoff. Heat not an issue. Altitude is
Winner faces Brazil/Norway winner in the quarterfinal. England's first game at the Azteca since the 1986 Maradona quarterfinal

sections.name: Team Comparison
rows: Compact, disciplined defense (zero goals conceded) with attacking width via Alvarado and Quinones
Possession control; plan is to slow the game to manage altitude fatigue and use subs late (subs decided the DR Congo game)
Won Group A with first-ever 3/3: 2-0 South Africa, 1-0 South Korea, 3-0 Czechia
Won Group L (7 pts): 4-2 Croatia, 0-0 Ghana, 2-0 Panama
2-0 Ecuador at the Azteca, snapped an 8-game WC knockout losing streak (longest ever); first knockout win since 1986
2-1 DR Congo, trailed early until a Kane double in the final 15 min; first WC win from behind since the 1966 final
4 wins from 4, 6 straight wins, 12-match unbeaten run
Unbeaten (W3 D1) but widely criticized as underwhelming and trending the wrong way
Julian Quinones: involved in 4 goals; one more goal/assist sets Mexico's single-WC record
Harry Kane: 5 goals, quietly in the Golden Boot race
Raul Jimenez, Roberto Alvarado, Gilberto Mora (17, 2nd-youngest ever to start a WC knockout match)
Bellingham, Saka, Anthony Gordon (first England player with multiple WC goal involvements as a sub)
Jude Bellingham: one more booking = miss the QF
Reece James (hamstring) not expected back; Quansah (ankle) major doubt; Rice played through hamstring tightness and is being pushed to play
Played June 30 at the Azteca; 5 days rest, ZERO travel, fully altitude-adjusted
Played July 1; 4 days rest plus travel to 2,200m altitude

sections.name: Head-To-Head
rows: England lead 6-2-1 in 9 meetings (23-4 aggregate), won the last four, last in 2010 (3-1 Wembley). England haven't lost to Mexico since 1985, but have never faced them at a packed competitive Azteca

sections.name: Boosts and Hindrances (Pros / Cons)
rows: Altitude (VO2max drops 10-13% for unacclimatized players, Tuchel admits a 'huge disadvantage'); never lost a WC match at the Azteca (8W 2D); 26-game unbeaten run at the ground; ~87,000 crowd; more rest, no travel; only team yet to concede
Superior individual talent; Kane's form (5 goals); strong H2H record; England's late-sub surges have won them games; better big-tournament experience
H2H hoodoo (no win vs England since 1985); haven't faced an attack of England's class yet; historic knockout fragility only just exorcised

Altitude + travel + one day less rest; defensive injuries (James, Quansah) forcing Rice out of position while carrying a knock; Bellingham card risk; no win from open-play dominance yet, performances declining

sections.name: Market and Expert View
rows: Moneyline: England +125, Mexico +245, Draw +220 (DraftKings/Covers). To advance: England -108, 'nearly a coin toss' (FOX). Racing Post leans Mexico angles on altitude; Sports Mole predicts 1-1, England on pens

sections.name: Final Verdict
rows: MEXICO 2-1 AFTER EXTRA TIME. The market slightly favors England, but every situational factor (2,200m altitude, a fortress Azteca where Mexico have never lost a WC game, extra rest, zero travel, four straight clean sheets) points the other way, and England arrive with a patched-up defense and fading performances. The upset pick of the round, by the narrowest margin
Sports Mole preview + injury list; Olympics.com altitude analysis; Racing Post; FOX Sports / DraftKings odds; 11v11 H2H; Squawka tracker

matches.rationale: The complete team vs Ronaldo's last stand; Spain's class in 90
matches.sheetTitle: Match 5: Spain vs Portugal, Mon July 6, 2026

sections.name: Prediction
rows: The Iberian derby: the tournament's most complete team against Ronaldo's last stand

sections.name: Match Info
rows: Monday, July 6, 2026, 2:00 PM local (CT) / 3:00 PM ET
Outside: ~36C (97F), mostly sunny, 30% storm chance, gusty south winds. Indoors: controlled conditions
Winner likely faces Belgium/USA winner in the quarterfinal. Widely expected to be Ronaldo's final World Cup

sections.name: Team Comparison
rows: Not confirmed in sources (Roberto Martinez unconfirmed)
Possession control + defensive solidity, four clean sheets; Rodri-Pedri axis, Yamal the biggest threat from the right
Built around Ronaldo as the focal point; Bruno Fernandes chief creator and set-piece threat; Neto/Leao wide
Unbeaten, no goals conceded: 0-0 Cape Verde, 4-0 Saudi Arabia, 1-0 Uruguay
1-1 DR Congo, 5-0 Uzbekistan, 0-0 Colombia. Hot and cold
2-1 Croatia (Toronto), stoppage-time winner (90+4); Croatia equalizer ruled out by VAR ball-chip technology
8 / 2, two labored draws, reliant on late heroics
Won three straight with four clean sheets
Unbeaten but unconvincing outside the Uzbekistan rout
Mikel Oyarzabal: 4 goals (joint top scorer), xG 3.19; plus Lamine Yamal (1 goal, xG 2.26)
Cristiano Ronaldo (41): 3 goals, match-high xG 4.15, chasing 1,000 career goals in his final World Cup
Bruno Fernandes, Vitinha, Joao Neves, Diogo Costa
Ruben Dias (elbow vs Croatia): one more booking = miss the QF
Rodri playing but reportedly needs post-tournament surgery, a watch-point; Carvajal missed the whole tournament
Both played July 2 (4 days rest); Spain travels from Los Angeles
Lost to Portugal on pens in the June 2025 Nations League final (missed one)
Beat Spain on pens in the 2025 NL final (converted 5/5); lost the Euro 2012 semi shootout to Spain 4-2

sections.name: Head-To-Head
rows: 41 meetings; friendlies dead even. Competitive edge to Spain: 2010 WC R16 Spain 1-0; Euro 2012 semi Spain on pens; 2018 WC 3-3 (Ronaldo hat-trick). Most recent: 2025 Nations League final 2-2, Portugal won 5-3 on pens

sections.name: Boosts and Hindrances (Pros / Cons)
rows: Best defense in the tournament (0 conceded in 4); most complete squad; model favorite (64%); no card risks; Oyarzabal + Yamal both in scoring form
Ronaldo's occasion and set-piece/xG threat; won the most recent meeting (2025 NL final, on pens); climate-controlled venue removes fatigue variance; knows how to win ugly late
Rodri managing an injury; lost the last shootout to this exact opponent; slight complacency risk after cruising
Ruben Dias on a yellow; defense conceding chances (2 goals, late-winner escapes); Spain's press targets an aging front line

sections.name: Market and Expert View
rows: bet365: Spain -111, Draw +250, Portugal +300; Under 2.5 -111. Squawka Signal model: Spain 64% / Portugal 36%; Spain 19% to win the whole tournament. Kalshi market: Spain 52 / Draw 27 / Portugal 24

sections.name: Final Verdict
rows: SPAIN 2-1. Spain are simply the better, fresher, more balanced side and haven't conceded all tournament. Portugal's clearest route is dragging it to penalties (where they beat Spain 13 months ago), so Spain's job is to avoid that. A goal each way, Spain's class tells in 90
Squawka preview + Signal model + suspension tracker; Sporting News/Yahoo H2H; Irish Examiner (Rodri, Ronaldo); NWS Arlington forecast

matches.rationale: Home crowd + fresher legs vs Belgium's 120-minute comeback hangover
matches.sheetTitle: Match 6: Belgium vs USA, Mon July 6, 2026

sections.name: Prediction
rows: 2014 revenge in Seattle: host energy and Belgium's 120-minute legs tip a genuine pick 'em

sections.name: Match Info
rows: Monday, July 6, 2026, 5:00 PM local (PT) / 8:00 PM ET, on FOX
No roof over the pitch (partial stand cover)
Near-perfect: 23-24C (73-75F), dry, light winds, no weather factor
Winner likely faces Spain/Portugal winner. Rematch of the 2014 WC R16 (Belgium 2-1 AET, De Bruyne and Lukaku scored, both still in this squad)

sections.name: Team Comparison
rows: 4-2-3-1 (has also used a back three this tournament)
Possession-based; wingers Doku and Trossard the biggest threats; Lukaku as super-sub; defense the weakest part
Energetic pressing, dangerous on set pieces (4 of 10 goals); Tillman/Pulisic creating, Pepi to lead the line
Won Group G: 1-1 Egypt, 0-0 Iran, 5-1 New Zealand
Won Group D: 4-1 Paraguay, 2-0 Australia, 2-3 Turkey
3-2 AET vs Senegal at THIS stadium, trailed 2-0 until the 86th; Tielemans penalty in the 125th min, the LATEST goal in WC history; only 2nd team ever to win a WC match from 2 down
2-0 Bosnia, Balogun goal then a VAR red card (cannot be appealed); Tillman free kick; held on with 10 men. First US knockout win in 24 years
9 / 4, sluggish early, 8 goals in the last 2 games
Riding a miracle comeback; defense still shaky
Momentum strong despite the Turkey loss
Kevin De Bruyne (34): still the creative hub; Romelu Lukaku: 2 goals in 2 games off the bench, record scorer (92)
Christian Pulisic: 0 goals, managing a calf issue, but the talisman the US needs firing with Balogun out
Youri Tielemans (captain, hero vs Senegal), Doku, Trossard, Courtois
Chris Richards (outstanding all tournament), Malik Tillman, Adams, McKennie, keeper Freese
Folarin Balogun (team top scorer, 3 goals): RED CARD ban; back for the QF if they advance
Brandon Mechele (starting CB); coach Rudi Garcia also on a yellow
Lukaku's match fitness limited, likely sub again
Pulisic playing through calf discomfort; McKenzie and Roldan questionable (non-starters)
Played July 1, but 120+ minutes; stayed in Seattle (same venue), zero travel
Played July 1 (90 min); 5 days rest, home-nation comfort
Nothing current-tournament confirmed for either side

sections.name: Head-To-Head
rows: Belgium won the 2014 WC R16 2-1 AET (Tim Howard's 15-save night). USA haven't beaten Belgium since the 1930 World Cup. Warning sign: Belgium 5-2 USA in a March 2026 friendly

sections.name: Boosts and Hindrances (Pros / Cons)
rows: More talent on paper (rank 9); emotional surge from the greatest comeback of the tournament; no travel, camped at this exact stadium; recent 5-2 friendly rout of this opponent
Massive home crowd in a passionate soccer city; fresher legs (90 min vs 120+); Belgium's defense is their weak link vs USA's set-piece threat; Balogun back for QF adds incentive to get there
120 extra-time minutes in the legs 5 days out; weakest-part defense; Mechele one card from a QF ban; aging spine (KDB 34, Lukaku 33 and not fully fit)
No Balogun (top scorer); Pulisic not fully fit and goalless; lost the March friendly 5-2; hasn't beaten Belgium in 96 years

sections.name: Market and Expert View
rows: Opened as a pick 'em (FOX). Moneyline: USA +165, Belgium +170, Draw +230; USMNT slight favorite to ADVANCE (Yahoo/SI)

sections.name: Final Verdict
rows: USA 2-1. The friendly rout in March flatters Belgium. Knockout football at a roaring Lumen Field, against extra-time legs and a shaky Belgian back line, is a different game. Tillman and the set-piece machine get it done in 90, with Pepi or Richards the unlikely hero. Thinnest of edges: Belgium's comeback DNA makes this the round's most volatile tie
Seattle Sounders previews; bet365 preview; Al Jazeera (Belgium comeback); ESPN (Balogun red, March friendly); FOX/Yahoo/SI odds; Squawka tracker

matches.rationale: Messi's Golden Boot form and a 10-game WC win streak vs a blunt attack
matches.sheetTitle: Match 7: Egypt vs Argentina, Tue July 7, 2026

sections.name: Prediction
rows: Messi vs Salah, once. The champions' 10-game WC win streak against Egypt's low block

sections.name: Match Info
rows: Atlanta Stadium (Mercedes-Benz Stadium), 75,000+ for the World Cup
Retractable roof (typically closed) with climate control, weather neutralized
Outside: ~35C (95F), humid, one of the month's rainiest days projected (thunderstorm risk). Irrelevant indoors
Winner faces Switzerland/Colombia winner. Billed as the last World Cup for BOTH Messi (39) and Salah (34)

sections.name: Team Comparison
rows: Flexible 4-3-3 (shifts to 4-4-2 / back five by game state)
Deep compact block, double pivot, rapid transitions releasing Salah (free role) and Marmoush
Possession control through Enzo Fernandez-Mac Allister-De Paul; Messi orchestrating
2nd in Group G: 1-1 Belgium, 3-1 New Zealand (first-ever WC win, at their 8th World Cup), 1-1 Iran
Won Group A (9 pts): 3-0 Algeria, 2-0 Austria, 3-1 Jordan
1-1 Australia, won 4-2 on penalties (Salah converted), first knockout win in Egypt's history
3-2 AET vs Cape Verde, a huge scare vs the lowest-ranked knockout team ever; Romero header from a Messi corner won it in the 111th
11 / 3, on a 10-match World Cup winning streak dating to 2022
W1 D3, no regulation losses; solid, low-event football
W4, but only ~1.67 xG in regulation vs Cape Verde; over-reliant on Messi for creation
Mohamed Salah: goal + assist vs NZ; created 5 chances vs Australia while double-marked; converted in the shootout; his last World Cup
Lionel Messi: GOLDEN BOOT LEADER, 7 goals in 4 games; became all-time WC top scorer (20 career) and first ever to score in 7 straight WC matches
Emam Ashour (R32 goal), Omar Marmoush, Mostafa 'Zico'
Lisandro Martinez and Romero (ET goals), Enzo Fernandez, Mac Allister
None (Lasheen served his ban in the R32; should return)
Salah recovered from a hamstring strain (Iran game), fitness worth monitoring after 120 minutes
Balerdi out, Senesi called up as replacement; otherwise intact
Both played 120 min on July 3 (4 days rest); Egypt adds shootout legs; travels Dallas to Atlanta
Just won their first-ever WC shootout
Elite: won shootouts vs Netherlands and France at Qatar 2022

sections.name: Head-To-Head
rows: Essentially one senior meeting, a 2008 friendly won by Argentina. Never met at a World Cup

sections.name: Boosts and Hindrances (Pros / Cons)
rows: Nothing to lose; defense unbeaten in 90 minutes all tournament; Salah's individual magic; roof removes heat that would punish a low block
Messi in historic form (7 goals); 10 straight WC wins; far deeper squad; elite shootout record if it gets there; noon kick suits the fresher, deeper side
Only 6 goals in 4 games, must score against the tournament's meanest big-game defense; shootout + 120 min in the legs; two defenders on yellows
Also played 120 minutes; regulation-time chance creation has dipped; Cape Verde exposed some fragility; Montiel card risk

sections.name: Market and Expert View
rows: bet365: Argentina -300, Draw +400, Egypt +750; Argentina -800 to reach QF. DraftKings: Argentina -270 / +370 / +800. BetMGM pick: Under 2.5 (-120) citing both teams' 120-minute legs

sections.name: Final Verdict
rows: ARGENTINA 2-0. Egypt's block keeps it tight for an hour, but Argentina's quality is relentless and Messi only needs one set piece or one moment. Egypt's blunt attack (6 goals in 4) means Argentina rarely look like conceding. Low-scoring, controlled, professional
Sky Sports match reports; FOX Golden Boot tracker; Squawka tracker; BetMGM/DraftKings odds; FIFA/ESPN match pages; AccuWeather Atlanta

matches.rationale: Better defense and tougher tests trump Switzerland's rest-and-venue edge
matches.sheetTitle: Match 8: Switzerland vs Colombia, Tue July 7, 2026

sections.name: Prediction
rows: Two unbeaten sides for the last QF spot: Colombia's higher ceiling vs Switzerland's rest-and-venue edge

sections.name: Match Info
rows: Tuesday, July 7, 2026, 1:00 PM local (PT) / 4:00 PM ET, the final R16 tie
Mild and comfortable: ~22C (72F), low rain likelihood, no weather factor
The final quarterfinal place, likely vs Egypt/Argentina winner

sections.name: Team Comparison
rows: Compact, congests central spaces, five-man midfield out of possession; attacks via wings (Ndoye, Vargas), Xhaka's vertical passing, crosses for Embolo
James as the No. 10, Diaz wide left; defensively excellent (1 conceded), patient control
Won Group B (7 pts): 1-1 Qatar, 4-1 Bosnia, 2-1 Canada
Won Group K (7 pts): 3-1 Uzbekistan, 1-0 DR Congo, 0-0 Portugal (1.7 xG despite the blank)
2-0 Algeria at BC Place (Embolo 10', Ndoye 46'), first clean sheet of their tournament
1-0 Ghana in Kansas City (Jhon Arias tap-in 14')
9 / 3, attack overperforming; defense rated 'semi-fraudulent' (conceded 1.7 xG to Canada)
Unbeaten (W3 D1), but yet to face a top-20 Elo opponent
Unbeaten (W3 D1), faced the stronger opposition (held Portugal)
Breel Embolo: opened vs Algeria, aerial focal point; Granit Xhaka the leader
Luis Diaz: goal + assist vs Uzbekistan, two disallowed vs DR Congo, the main threat
Dan Ndoye, Ruben Vargas, Widmer/Rodriguez crossing
James Rodriguez (now Colombia's all-time WC appearance leader, but subbed early every match), Jhon Arias, Daniel Munoz, Luis Suarez
Jhon Arias and Richard Rios: one more booking = miss the QF
None reported; only caveat is James's repeated early withdrawals
Played July 2 AT BC PLACE, 5 days rest, zero travel
Played July 3 in Kansas City, a day less rest plus a cross-continent flight
Neither side needed extra time in the R32, both fresh on that front

sections.name: Head-To-Head
rows: Only prior meeting: 1994 World Cup group stage, Colombia 2-0 Switzerland (Gaviria, Lozano)

sections.name: Boosts and Hindrances (Pros / Cons)
rows: Slept in Vancouver all week, same venue, 5 days rest, no travel; unbeaten; strong set-piece and crossing game vs a Colombia side that scores little
Best defense they will have faced (1 conceded, 3 clean sheets); higher-quality opposition faced (Portugal); Diaz in form; won the only H2H; bookmakers' favorite
Untested vs elite (no top-20 Elo opponent yet); defense concedes chances; Colombia's press-resistant midfield is a step up
Travel + shorter rest (flagged by BetMGM as a real disadvantage); only 5 goals in 4 games; Arias and Rios on yellows; James's fitness limits the creative spell to ~60 min

sections.name: Market and Expert View
rows: BetMGM: Colombia +120 favorite, Draw +225, Switzerland +220; BTTS Yes -105 (their pick, Swiss defense untested, Colombia's travel spot). Nate Silver's model: Colombia favored in a weak bracket slot

sections.name: Final Verdict
rows: COLOMBIA 2-1. Switzerland's rest-and-venue edge is real and keeps this tight, but Colombia have faced tougher opponents, defend far better, and have the two best attackers on the pitch in Diaz and Arias. Colombia's first quarterfinal since 2014, with the caveat that this is the closest call after Mexico-England
